2605.25846.title: On the Limits of Model Merging for Multilinguality in Pre-Training
2605.25846.paper_summary: Endowing models with consistent multilingual performance can be achieved by mixing pre-training data, or post-training approaches such as language-specific model merging. In this work, we test whether merging can be applied to monolingually pre-trained models. We conduct a controlled study on the efficacy of mixed, merged, and monolingual pre-training setups. We find that while monolingual pre-training results in strong in-language performance, merging any combination of monolingual models leads to performance collapse due to interference. Our analysis suggests representational similarity is a prerequisite for model merging. We therefore conclude that the flexibility of merging in fine-tuning does not extend trivially to language-specific pre-training.
2605.25846.paper_summary_zh: 賦予模型一致的多語言表現可以通過混合預訓練數據或後訓練方法，如語言特定模型合併來實現。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們測試了合併是否可以應用於單語預訓練模型。<lb/>我們對混合、合併和單語預訓練設置的有效性進行了控制研究。<lb/>我們發現，雖然單語預訓練在語言內表現上表現強勁，但合併任何組合的單語模型會因干擾而導致性能崩潰。<lb/>我們的分析表明，表示相似性是模型合併的前提條件。<lb/>因此，我們得出結論，合併在微調中的靈活性並不會輕易擴展到語言特定的預訓練。
2605.25846.author: Seth Aycock et.al.
2605.25846.authors: Seth Aycock, Fedor Vitiugin, Aleksandr Umnov, Christof Monz, Khalil Sima'an
